_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MS/DNB or Equivalent (in Urology/ Pediatric Surgery)
clinical_indications: Malignancy/Suspicion of Malignancy: Adrenal masses, Neuroblastoma (Paediatric population), Large myelolipoma, Adrenocortical carcinoma. In rare conditions- Cushing's syndrome, Conn syndrome etc.
Tumor diameter: >6 cm ACC with no local invasion
Patients with suspected or large adrenocortical tumors (>6–8 cm)

clinical_thresholds.note: for suspected or large adrenocortical tumors, warrants open surgery

preauth.label: Detailed Clinical notes with history, indications, symptoms, signs, examination findings and advice for admission
Sr. Cortisol, Sr. electrolytes, 24 Hr. Urinary catecholamine level/Sr. Urinary metanephrine normetanephrine

ppd_preauth.q: Detailed Clinical notes – all vitals, history signs & symptoms, physical examination including local examination, indication for procedure, advise for admission, and planned line of treatment?
Was CT/MRI +/- FNAC/Biopsy/ MIBG (iodine meta-iodobenzylguanidine) scan/ DOTONOC scan reports submitted?
Was Serum Cortisol, Serum electrolytes, 24 Hr. Urinary catecholamine level/Serum Urinary metanephrine normetanephrine submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details available?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes and CT/MRI/MIBG scan/DOTONOC scan report not indicative of surgery.
Detailed Clinical notes missing essential information (vitals, history, indications, symptoms, signs, examination findings, advice for admission, or planned line of treatment).
Required imaging reports (CT/MRI/FNAC/Biopsy/MIBG/DOTONOC) not submitted at pre-authorization.
Required laboratory reports (Sr. Cortisol, Sr. electrolytes, 24 Hr. Urinary catecholamine level/Sr. Urinary metanephrine normetanephrine) not submitted at pre-authorization.
Detailed indoor case papers with daily vitals and treatment details not available at claim submission.
Detailed procedure / operative notes not available at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Though minimally invasive techniques for adrenalectomy have been adopted in many surgical centers, there is a need to be, remain or become confident with open adrenalectomy.
contraindications: Laparoscopic surgery is contraindicated (requiring open surgery)
